title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set
input_row.description: Limit the Node and Pod objects that a kubelet could modify.
input_row.rationale: Using the NodeRestriction plug-in ensures that the kubelet is restricted to the Node and Pod objects that it could modify as defined. Such kubelets will only be allowed to modify their own Node API object, and only modify Pod API objects that are bound to their node.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes NodeRestriction .
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure NodeRestriction plug-in on kubelets. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the -- enable-admission-plugins parameter to a value that includes NodeRestriction . --enable-admission-plugins=...,NodeRestriction,...
input_row.default_value: By default, NodeRestriction is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugins_required_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'` to retrieve the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- Check if the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument includes `NodeRestriction`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `--enable-admission-plugins` argument contains `NodeRestriction`.
  - **Fail:** The `--enable-admission-plugins` argument does not contain `NodeRestriction` or is not set at all.
